Accepts a limited number of peer review assignments per semester — declines additional requests to protect research time
Never reads or responds to work emails after 8 pm — strictly silences notifications to preserve home time
Hosts Sunday family dinners each week — screens all personal calls during this time
Uses shared digital calendars with her husband to coordinate childcare and errands — never relies on verbal reminders
Limits volunteering to the school library only — declines other school-related volunteer opportunities
Tries a brand new vegetarian recipe with her son every weekend — never repeats the same dish in a given month
Spends Saturday afternoons reading modern fiction — never reads work-related material during leisure time
Enjoys jigsaw puzzles with her son after dinner — avoids screen-based games in the evening
Preps a to-do list every morning before opening her laptop — never starts digital work without this ritual
Checks news headlines only with her first cup of coffee — avoids reading news in the evening
Reviews her week and plans major tasks every Sunday night — refrains from scheduling anything for Monday evenings
Coordinates grocery shopping for her mother with her sisters and son, often delegating shopping tasks and deliveries as needed due to increased caregiving demands
Now participates in frequent in-person meetings with interdisciplinary faculty twice weekly as a co-chair, supplementing asynchronous communication with direct collaboration
Reserves Wednesday afternoons for preparatory work and reflection ahead of in-person initiative meetings, treating this as non-negotiable focus time
Regularly connects with faculty from other departments over coffee after planning meetings to foster interdisciplinary relationships
Proactively arranges video check-ins with her husband and son after his late meetings to maintain evening family connection
Blocks Monday mornings primarily for onboarding, team recruitment, and urgent grant-related meetings — rarely protects this time for uninterrupted writing during project rollout
Prioritizes weekly community partnership meetings during the first months of the grant rollout — automatically reschedules less critical internal team catch-ups to accommodate these
Dedicates one evening per week to developing a public outreach plan, separating this work from regular research and teaching duties
Prefers to delegate routine research administration and clerical grant tasks to newly hired staff whenever possible — no longer manages these details herself
Prepares fresh plant-based low-sodium dinners five nights a week — avoids all processed foods and never orders delivery, even on special occasions
Reads nutrition labels meticulously for all packaged ingredients, excluding anything with added sodium or preservatives from home meals
Began batch-cooking unsalted, plant-based staples on Sundays to simplify weeknight dinners and avoid resorting to processed foods
Relies on daily video or phone check-ins with her mother while abroad, delegating in-person visits to her sisters
Explores independent European bookstores and cafes near her host university, avoiding chain establishments while abroad
Schedules weekly video calls with her husband and son to maintain family connection during her semester abroad
Participates in cultural outings organized by her host university, preferring group excursions to solo sightseeing while abroad
Plans frequent in-person conversations and activities with her sister in Ann Arbor, replacing scheduled Sunday evening catch-up calls while sharing caregiving duties
Returns to silencing phone notifications in the evening, trusting her sister to handle urgent calls regarding their mother's health while she's home
Designates at least two shared family evenings per week for collaborative caregiving and meal prep with her sister and mother, integrating her sister's participation as a new routine
Reserves a dedicated workspace in her home for her sister, ensuring quiet hours during weekday afternoons so both can focus on remote work responsibilities
Handles sole dinner and daytime supervision for her son on Fridays, preferring simple meals and quiet activities throughout the day due to the lack of school and her husband's varying work schedule
Blocks Friday mornings from university meetings and office hours, reserving this time exclusively for at-home supervision and activities with her son
Schedules collaborative planning sessions with her husband each Thursday evening to coordinate logistics for Friday at-home childcare and work arrangements
Ceases all group fitness classes—including yoga—temporarily and practices only gentle, doctor-approved restorative stretches at home until cleared by her cardiologist
Sets a nightly alarm to begin winding down for bed by 9:00 pm — strictly avoids all caffeine, even in the morning, as required by cardiologist
Schedules frequent short rest periods during the workday and at home to minimize fatigue and heart palpitations, never committing to back-to-back obligations
Brings a portable heart rate monitor and medication to work, checking vitals before and after meetings to proactively manage atrial fibrillation symptoms
Pauses all daily walks and outdoor family strolls, substituting with slow indoor movement and gentle activities, strictly avoiding outdoor walks on doctor's orders while wearing the heart monitor
Suspends family day trips during school breaks, opting instead for at-home activities and virtual experiences due to medical restrictions on travel while the heart monitor is in use
Carefully chooses only loose, comfortable clothing that accommodates electrode placement and avoids high-neck or tight tops while wearing the heart monitor
Schedules all necessary showers and hair washes for the evenings, using disposable cleansing wipes in the mornings to comply with monitor water exposure restrictions

superseded_preferences.fact: Blocks Monday mornings for uninterrupted writing — never schedules meetings or mentoring during this time
superseded_preferences.reason: The new federal grant requires immediate recruitment, onboarding, and community partnership-building within a tight schedule. She must now spend Monday mornings on team growth and grant-related activities, no longer on undisturbed writing.

superseded_preferences.fact: Prefers asynchronous communication with her research team — rarely calls or holds impromptu meetings
superseded_preferences.reason: The university restructuring mandates twice-weekly in-person planning meetings and sustained close collaboration with faculty from other departments, directly overriding her previous preference for asynchronous-only communication.

superseded_preferences.fact: Prepares fresh vegetarian dinners five nights a week — never orders delivery except for special occasions
superseded_preferences.reason: Her doctor's instructions call for a strict plant-based, low-sodium diet with immediate elimination of processed foods and takeout, directly contradicting her previous 'vegetarian' and 'special occasion delivery' exceptions.

superseded_preferences.fact: Takes daily 30-minute walks at lunchtime, regardless of weather — avoids evening workouts due to fatigue
superseded_preferences.reason: Her husband's new remote-only promotion requires frequent evening meetings across time zones, making it necessary for her to adjust routines to accommodate family obligations like childcare and dog walking. As a result, she now prefers evening walks with her son and dog during her husband's work calls.

superseded_preferences.fact: Attends in-person yoga classes every Thursday morning — never participates in virtual yoga sessions
superseded_preferences.reason: While serving as a visiting scholar in Europe, she will be unable to attend her regular in-person yoga classes with her neighbor, necessitating a shift to virtual sessions.

superseded_preferences.fact: Sets a nightly alarm as a cue to begin winding down for bed by 10:00 pm — refrains from caffeine after 2:00 pm
superseded_preferences.reason: Her atrial fibrillation diagnosis and physician’s instructions require more sleep and elimination of stimulants to manage arrhythmia risk; thus, she now begins winding down earlier and excludes all caffeine.

superseded_preferences.fact: Avoids all high-impact exercise since her ankle injury — only chooses low-impact or restorative activities for now
superseded_preferences.reason: The diagnosis of atrial fibrillation and recent symptom episode during yoga mean she must now fully avoid even low-impact group classes like yoga due to safety concerns and medical advice, shifting exclusively to gentle home routines until cleared.

superseded_preferences.fact: Schedules all catch-up calls with out-of-state sisters for Sunday evenings — never does impromptu calls
superseded_preferences.reason: With her sister now living locally for six months and actively involved in caregiving, maintaining the old ritual of scheduled Sunday evening calls is unnecessary. Their interactions shift to spontaneous and regular in-person contact.

superseded_preferences.fact: Shops for her mother every Saturday morning — never delegates this responsibility, even when busy
superseded_preferences.reason: After her mother’s stroke and need for ongoing in-home support, shopping for her mother is now more frequent and complex. She can't always do it alone, and must share or delegate this responsibility.

superseded_preferences.fact: Visits a new Ann Arbor bookstore or local coffee shop each month — avoids chain cafes entirely
superseded_preferences.reason: She will be living abroad for a semester, so monthly visits to Ann Arbor shops or cafes are not feasible.

superseded_preferences.fact: Plans a day trip for the family every school break — never spends the whole break at home
superseded_preferences.reason: The instructions to refrain from travel (even short trips) while being monitored mean she can no longer plan or take family outings during this period; instead, she must choose home-based or virtual alternatives.

superseded_preferences.fact: Shops for groceries strictly on Saturday mornings with her son — never does midweek grocery runs
superseded_preferences.reason: Her mother's frequent in-home support needs now require unpredictable grocery runs beyond Saturdays; the strict weekly routine is no longer possible.

superseded_preferences.fact: Walks the family dog before breakfast every weekday, enlisting her son as a helper — never skips except for severe weather
superseded_preferences.reason: Her absence abroad means she cannot perform morning dog walks in Ann Arbor; the routine becomes irrelevant for her.

superseded_preferences.fact: Makes daily check-in visits to her mother’s home before starting work — never skips a day unless traveling
superseded_preferences.reason: Her physical absence during the semester in Europe makes daily in-person visits impossible; she will now connect remotely and delegate local caregiving.

superseded_preferences.fact: Keeps her phone unmuted all day for urgent calls about her mother’s health — pauses notification silencing until her mother’s recovery stabilizes
superseded_preferences.reason: Her sister's presence and shared caregiving means the urgency for instant response lessens; she can once again silence notifications outside caregiving hours, relying on her sister as primary point of contact.

superseded_preferences.fact: Shifts daily 30-minute walks to the early evening with her son and dog to coincide with her husband's evening work meetings — no longer avoids evening walks if family schedule requires it
superseded_preferences.reason: The physician's restrictions with the wearable heart monitor explicitly ban even moderate outdoor walks, requiring her to halt the former routine and replace it with only gentle, doctor-approved indoor activities until heart rhythm tracking is complete.

superseded_preferences.fact: Handles sole dinner and bedtime routines with her son on weeknights when her husband is in meetings, and prefers simple meals and quiet activities during these evenings
superseded_preferences.reason: With the elementary school moving to a four-day in-person schedule, she will now need to manage at-home supervision of her son every Friday—an additional responsibility beyond evenings, requiring adjustments to her routine and preferences on Fridays.

superseded_preferences.fact: Joins virtual yoga sessions from her host university while abroad — pauses in-person classes for the semester
superseded_preferences.reason: She is no longer abroad and, due to atrial fibrillation, cannot participate in any yoga (virtual or in-person) until cleared by her cardiologist; the context for this preference has fully changed.
